title: TC001-Admin dashboard shows overview metric cards after successful login
description: Verifies that the admin dashboard loads and displays the four overview metric cards.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the username field with 'nandika' (then fill password and click Sign in).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('nandika')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('klp123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert "/admin" in frame.url
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside[1]/div/nav/div[2]/ul/li[2]/a')
        assert await elem.is_visible()
        # The following features were not found in the provided available elements list; report and mark task as done
        missing = []
        missing.append('Active Bookings')
        missing.append('Total Bookings')
        if missing:
            raise AssertionError('Missing features on page: ' + ', '.join(missing))
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

        frame = context.pages[-1]

        frame = context.pages[-1]

        # --> Assertions to verify final state

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC009-View bookings summary cards and bookings table after login
description: Verifies the Events page loads after login and shows summary cards and the bookings table structure.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill username and password, then click the Sign in button to log in.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('nandika')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('klp123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click 'Events' in the admin navigation (element index 454) to open the Events page and then verify the required assertions.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/div/nav/div/ul/li[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Events').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Total Bookings').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator("xpath=//table//thead//tr[1][contains(., 'Client') and contains(., 'Package') and contains(., 'Session Date') and contains(., 'Status') and contains(., 'Galleries')]").first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC011-Create a new booking from Events page and see it in the table
description: Verifies an admin can create a booking and then see the new booking appear in the bookings table.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type username and password into the login form and click the 'Sign in' button to log in as admin.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('nandika')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('klp123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click 'Events' in the admin navigation to open the Events page (use element index 493).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/div/nav/div/ul/li[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click 'Events' in the admin navigation (index 493) to open the Events page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/div/nav/div/ul/li[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate directly to /admin/events (fallback) to reach the Events page, because clicking the Events nav did not navigate.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/admin/events", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the '+ Create event' button to open the create event form (element index 1315).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/header/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Input 'Test Client A' into the Client Name field (index 1539) and then click the Create Booking button (index 1593) to submit the booking.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div/div/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Test Client A')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div/div/form/div[9]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Fill the missing required fields (Phone, Session Date, Location) in the Create Booking modal and submit the booking, so the bookings table can be checked for 'Test Client A'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div/div/form/div/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('+6281234567890')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div/div/form/div[3]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('2026-04-18')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div/div/form/div[3]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Gedung Serbaguna')
        
        # -> Click the 'Create Booking' submit button to submit the booking (element index 1593). After submission, verify the new booking appears in the bookings table for 'Test Client A'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div/div/form/div[9]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Verify we are on the Events page
        assert "/admin/events" in frame.url
        
        # Verify the newly created booking for 'Test Client A' appears in the bookings table (client link in row 1)
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div[3]/table/tbody/tr[1]/td[2]/div/a').is_visible()
        
        # Verify the Total Bookings widget is visible
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div[2]/div[1]').is_visible()
        
        # Verify action links/buttons for the booking row are present (Detail link and Pay button)
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div[3]/table/tbody/tr[1]/td[8]/div/a[1]').is_visible()
        assert await frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div[3]/table/tbody/tr[1]/td[8]/div/button').is_visible()
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC012-Submitting create booking with missing required fields shows validation errors
description: Verifies the create booking form prevents submission when required fields are missing and shows validation errors.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the username and password fields with the provided credentials and click the 'Sign in' button to log in.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('nandika')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('klp123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click 'Events' in the admin navigation to open the Events page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/div/nav/div/ul/li[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Ensure the Events page is rendered by clicking the 'Events' navigation item again and wait for the SPA to update so the '+ Create event' button can be located.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/div/nav/div/ul/li[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate directly to http://localhost:3000/admin/events to load the Events page so the '+ Create event' button can be located and clicked.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/admin/events", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the '+ Create Event' button to open the create event form so the submit validation can be tested.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/header/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Create Booking' (submit) button to trigger validation, then search the page for the text 'required' to verify validation errors are displayed.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div/div/form/div[9]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div[1]/div/form/div[9]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(1000)
        assert await elem.is_visible(), 'Create Booking (submit) button is not visible'
        # The test plan expects a validation message with the text "required" after submitting the empty form.
        # No element containing the text "required" exists in the provided Available elements list, so we cannot assert its visibility.
        raise AssertionError("Validation message 'required' not found in available elements; feature may be missing or uses a different message")
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC016-Create/Upload new gallery entry point is available from Galleries page
description: Verifies the user can initiate the new gallery workflow from the Galleries page and sees a create/upload UI entry point.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the username and password fields and click the Sign in button to log in (input index 7 -> 'nandika', input index 8 -> 'klp123', then click index 9).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('nandika')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('klp123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Galleries' link in the admin navigation to open the Galleries page (use interactive element index 475).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/div/nav/div[2]/ul/li[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate directly to the Galleries page at /admin/galleries to open the Galleries UI so the New Gallery workflow entry point can be located.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/admin/galleries", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Project').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Create').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- New Gallery button not found on Galleries page.
- No visible create/upload entry point labeled 'Create' or 'Project' present on the Galleries page.
- New gallery workflow cannot be initiated because the Galleries UI lacks an entry point to create/upload a gallery.

title: TC018-Create gallery with project name and verify it appears in list
description: Verifies creating a gallery with a project name results in the new gallery being listed on the Galleries page.

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():

    browser = None
    context = None

title: TC028-All settings groups are visible on Admin Settings page

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill username and password and click the 'Sign in' button to log in. After the page navigates to the admin area, click 'Settings' in the admin navigation.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('nandika')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('klp123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click 'Settings' in the admin navigation to open the Settings page and verify 'Studio Profile', 'Access Control', and 'Notifications' are visible along with their Configure actions.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/div/nav/div[3]/ul/li/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Settings' link in the admin navigation to open the Settings page, then verify the three groups ('Studio Profile', 'Access Control', 'Notifications') and their Configure actions. Immediate action: click Settings link (index 444).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/div/nav/div[2]/ul/li[3]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Verify 'Studio Profile' section is visible
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div/div[1]')
        assert await elem.is_visible(), "Studio Profile section is not visible on Settings page"
        # Verify 'Studio Profile' Configure button is visible
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div/div[1]/button')
        assert await elem.is_visible(), "Configure button for Studio Profile is not visible"
        # Check that 'Access Control' and 'Notifications' texts exist on the page; if not, report the issue and stop
        body = await frame.content()
        missing = []
        if 'Access Control' not in body:
            missing.append('Access Control')
        if 'Notifications' not in body:
            missing.append('Notifications')
        if missing:
            raise AssertionError('Missing settings groups on Settings page: ' + ', '.join(missing))
        # Verify Configure buttons for the other two settings groups are visible
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div/div[2]/button')
        assert await elem.is_visible(), "Configure button for the second settings group is not visible"
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/div/div/section/div/div[3]/button')
        assert await elem.is_visible(), "Configure button for the third settings group is not visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

        assert '/admin' in frame.url
        assert '/admin/clients' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//aside//nav//a[contains(normalize-space(.),"Clients") and (contains(@class,"active") or @aria-current="page")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC035-Navigate from Clients to Galleries via navigation and see active highlight
description: Verifies navigation can switch between sections and updates the current/active page indicator.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the username into the username field (index 7).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('nandika')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('klp123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Clients' navigation link in the sidebar (index 445) to navigate to the Clients page and then verify the URL contains '/admin/clients'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/div/nav/div[2]/ul/li[3]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Galleries' navigation link (index 441) to attempt navigation to the Galleries page and then verify the URL updates to contain '/admin/galleries'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/div/nav/div[2]/ul/li[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Galleries' navigation link (use index 437) to attempt navigation to the Galleries page so the URL and active state can be verified.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/aside/div/nav/div[2]/ul/li/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/admin/clients' in frame.url
        assert '/admin/galleries' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//a[normalize-space(text())="Galleries" and (contains(@class,"active") or @aria-current="page")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Clients navigation link click did not navigate to '/admin/clients' after multiple attempts.
- Clicking the Galleries navigation link did not consistently update the URL to '/admin/galleries'.
- The Galleries navigation link can appear visually active while the browser route does not match the active state.
- Sidebar navigation does not reliably trigger client-side route changes (SPA routing appears broken).
